name: Python sample-level cell composition
description: Aggregate cell-level or counted long data within biological samples and plot one stacked proportion bar per sample without treating cells as replicates.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: Cell-level rows, AnnData .obs, or counted long rows; caller data are not modified.

parameters.name: sample
parameters.description: Biological sample/donor identifier; each sample must map to exactly one group.

parameters.name: cell_type
parameters.description: Cell annotation column used for the stack.

parameters.name: count
parameters.description: Optional non-negative count column for pre-aggregated long data; otherwise rows are counted.

transformations.method: sum within sample x group x cell_type, complete absent categories with zero, divide by each sample total

supports_claims: Shows the supplied within-sample composition and between-sample heterogeneity while preserving biological replicates.
does_not_support: Does not by itself establish a condition effect, compositional differential abundance, causality, or equal sampling depth.
required_statistics: sample-level effect and uncertainty or a composition-aware model for inference
preferred_companions: sample-level dot/interval panel for a prespecified cell type
safer_alternatives: sample-level dot and interval plot when one prespecified cell type is the primary endpoint

risks: middle stack segments are hard to compare
visual_fingerprint.final_size_behavior: check rotated sample labels, legend and narrow stack segments
visual_fingerprint.review_note: Counted-long fixture rendered with count='count' and opened at original and 120 x 80 mm final size on 2026-07-17; axes, legend, sample labels, condition separator, and stacks were readable. The first omitted-count smoke was rejected because it treated aggregate rows as unit counts.

notes: Fixture covers counted long-table and AnnData-compatible .obs inputs.
Execution preserves individual samples and rejects samples mapped to multiple groups.
Hash-bound image_code_data review passed for original and final-size renders when the pre-aggregated count column was explicitly bound.
